summary: The session focused on the Intrinsic Motivation Inventory (IMI), a self-report measure developed by Richard M. Ryan and Edward L. Deci in 1985 to assess intrinsic motivation across seven dimensions. The user sought clarification on the number of dimensions in the IMI, confirming that it consists of seven rather than six.
facts: The Intrinsic Motivation Inventory (IMI) was developed by Richard M. Ryan and Edward L. Deci in 1985. The IMI assesses intrinsic motivation across seven dimensions: Interest/Enjoyment, Perceived Competence, Effort/Importance, Perceived Choice, Value/Usefulness, Relatedness, and Pressure/Tension. Respondents of the IMI rate items on a 7-point scale to measure their intrinsic motivation. The IMI has been widely used in research across various domains, including education, sports, and work. The IMI has demonstrated good reliability and validity in assessing intrinsic motivation.
